reasoning: The flagged 'autoload.sh' script is not executed during PKGBUILD build/install functions. It's a documentation script unused in AUR packaging context, making qwen's finding a false positive.
disagreement_analysis: qwen incorrectly flagged an inert documentation script as unsafe due to theoretical risks, while deepseek missed source code review entirely.
coverage_issues: deepseek skipped all source files (only reviewed PKGBUILD)
Neither model reviewed build hooks like prepare()/build() in PKGBUILD
Critical scripts executed during 'make' (e.g., Kbuild scripts) were not examined
false_positives: autoload.sh: Documentation script never executed during makepkg; theoretical injection risk irrelevant to AUR build
false_negatives: No review of kernel build scripts (e.g., Kbuild) that actually execute during compilation
PKGBUILD's prepare()/build() functions containing patching/compilation logic not analyzed
re_audit_focus: Source files referenced in PKGBUILD prepare()/build()
learnings: Prioritize files referenced in PKGBUILD functions (prepare/build/package) over documentation
Improve context awareness: Flag only scripts executed during makepkg
Detect build-system scripts (Makefiles, Kbuild) as high-priority targets
Prevent skipping source archives when they contain build-executed scripts
Add verification step: 'Is this script invoked during build?' for findings
